data.title: Track this: Strava is laying the groundwork for an IPO
data.description:
Strava appears to be laying the groundwork for an IPO. Reports indicate that the San Francisco-based company is hearing pitches from Goldman Sachs (GS) and JPMorgan (JPM) to be its underwriters for a public debut. The timing of an official IPO filing is uncertain, although sources told The Information that the company has been meeting with prospective investors. Late last year, Strava CEO Michael Martin confirmed to the Financial Times that the intention was to go public at some point.

Strava is a privately held social fitness platform founded in 2009 by Mark Gainey and Michael Horvath that lets runners, cyclists, and other athletes track activities and share them with a large online community. Over the year, Strava has invested in richer mapping, AI-assisted route recommendations, points of interest, and advanced performance tools. The company has also moved deeper into coaching and training through acquisitions such as Runna (running plans) and The Breakaway (cycling training).

Notably, Strava reported having over 180 million total registered users in the company's 2025 Year in Sport report.

On a loose basis, companies that compete with Strava through hardware, apps, and sportswear include Peloton Interactive (PTON), Planet Fitness (PLNT), Apple (AAPL),  Garmin (GRMN), Nike (NKE), Adidas (ADDYY), and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL).

data.title: uniQure gains as FDA sets up meeting on gene therapy
data.description:
• uniQure (QURE) added ~14% in the premarket on Friday after the Dutch biotech announced that the U.S. FDA has scheduled a meeting to discuss a marketing application for its gene therapy candidate, AMT-130.
  • In November, the company disclosed that its plans to submit a Biologics License Application for AMT-130 against a neurodegenerative disorder called Huntington’s disease remain uncertain, given the regulatory feedback it received following a pre-BLA meeting.
  • However, during the newly scheduled Type A meeting, the FDA is expected to discuss the BLA data package that could support a potential accelerated approval for AMT-130, uniQure (QURE) said.
  • “We look forward to a constructive discussion with the FDA as we work toward a timely resolution regarding an accelerated approval pathway for AMT-130,” CEO Matt Kapusta added.

data.title: Appian to press IP claims against Pegaystems after court ruling; Pegasystems 'vindicated'
data.description:
Appian (APPN) said late on Thursday that it intends to appeal its press trade secret claims against Pegaystems (PEGA) after the Supreme Court of Virginia remanded the case for retrial and rejected Pega's arguments seeking to dismiss the case entirely. Conversely, Pegasystems said it feels “vindicated” with the ruling.

“None of the issues addressed by the Supreme Court of Virginia go to the principal facts regarding Pega's misappropriation of Appian trade secrets.  We will present that evidence to a new jury and remain confident our claims will be properly addressed by Virginia courts,” stated Jaye Campbell, General Counsel of Appian, in a statement.

The Supreme Court of Virginia's opinion described that Pega had hired a “spy” to obtain Appian information. The spy also went to “great lengths” to conceal his identity, used Appian's information for Pega's benefit and was able to gain access to Appian software through the use of aliases and non-Pega-related credentials. 

On the flip side, Pegasystems COO and CFO Kenneth Stillwell wrote an email to employees and said the company was “vindicated” by the ruling. Stillwell said the Supreme Court of Virginia agreed with the appellate court that the $2B verdict against it was “fundamentally flawed.”

The entire email can be seen below.

I’m excited to share another major win in our trade secret litigation with Appian. Today, the Supreme Court of Virginia issued a judgment unanimously affirming what the appellate court previously recognized – that the trial and resulting $2 billion verdict were fundamentally flawed. We’ve consistently said we’re confident we’d be vindicated in a fair trial, and today’s result takes us one step closer to that outcome.
 
As you may recall, in 2024, a Virginia appellate court unanimously vacated the flawed trade secrets trial, overturned the resulting $2 billion verdict and ordered a new trial. Appian then appealed to the Supreme Court of Virginia, and we countered by raising our own issues for appeal.
 
The Supreme Court’s decision today rejected every aspect of Appian’s appeal from the intermediate court. But, as expected, Appian is already spinning today’s opinion (and rehashing events only related to the $1 claim we chose not to appeal) even though the trade secrets trial is completely reversed. Make no mistake: today’s decision reinforces our position that there was significant error including:
 
1. Giving the jury an instruction that improperly shifted the burden of proof on damages to Pega.
2. Limiting Pega's ability to present evidence in response to Appian's requested damages.
3. Preventing Pega from showing evidence that its software did not incorporate Appian's alleged trade secrets.
4. Telling the jury that evidence about how many people had access to Appian’s trade secrets was “not relevant.”
 
The Supreme Court wasn’t comfortable weighing in on the existence of trade secrets, as they allowed that to be a question for a new jury after an error-free trial. But they did state that even the old trial evidence didn’t ensure a favorable verdict in Appian’s favor.
 
I want to express my gratitude for your continued focus and professionalism throughout this process. We’ve consistently delivered on our goals and provided innovative solutions to clients and partners – thanks to your dedication.
 
As we look ahead, we will continue to do what we do best – help our clients drive true transformation in their business!!  
 
Best,
 
Ken 

  •

data.title: iBio announces private placement financing to raise ~$26M
data.description:
iBio (IBIO) on Friday announced a securities purchase agreement with existing institutional investors for a private placement financing to raise ~$26M.

Shares were +16.17% pre-market to $2.73.

The biotech company is selling 11.06M shares of common stock at $2.35 per share.

The offering is expected to close on or about January 13, 2026.

The financing was led by Frazier Life Sciences, and is expected to extend the company's cash runway into 2028.

Net proceeds are intended to be used to advance the San Diego-based company's preclinical cardiometabolic programs, and to fund working capital requirements and for other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Aquestive drops as FDA flags issues in Anaphylm marketing application
data.description:
Aquestive Therapeutics (AQST) lost ~48% in the premarket on Friday after the company said that the U.S. FDA has identified deficiencies in its new drug application for its allergy therapy, Anaphylm, which is currently undergoing regulatory review.

In a letter to the company, the FDA did not specify the issues but noted they will impact labeling discussions and post-marketing commitments while its review remains ongoing.

The Warren, New Jersey-based biopharma said that it is in contact with the regulator to get more details about the deficiencies to address them before the target action date on Jan. 31

Additionally, Aquestive (AQST) reported unaudited cash and cash equivalents of roughly $120M as of Dec. 31 and disclosed plans to seek regulatory approval for Anaphylm in Canada, Europe, and the UK this year.

data.title: Greenbrier declares $0.32 dividend
data.description:
• Greenbrier (GBX) declares $0.32/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.4%
  • Payable Feb. 17; for shareholders of record Jan. 27; ex-div Jan. 27.
  • See GBX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bullish trading volume drops 25% M/M in December as bitcoin volume slides 33%
data.description:
Digital asset platform Bullish's (BLSH) total trading volume in December 2025 dropped 25% to $61.1B from $80.8B in November, as bitcoin trading volume fell 33% to $25.9B from $38.4B, the company said on Friday.

During that period, bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price fell by 3.2% to ~$87.5K. Bitcoin's monthly average volatility fell to 39% from 45% in November, Bullish said.

While the trading volume was significant, it wasn't the lowest monthly volume of the year. That was $39.6B in September, which was also the weakest volume for bitcoin, at $16.4B.

Average trading spread slipped to 1.71 basis points from 1.85 bps. The narrowest trading spread of the year was 1.32 bps in May.

For ethereum (ETH-USD), December trading volume fell to $9.6B from $14.1B, and its monthly average volatility fell to 39% from 45% in November. Like bitcoin, it's lowest trading volume was in September, at $8.8B.

Bullish (BLSH) stock rose 0.3% in premarket trading.

data.title: Athira Pharma changes name, ticker symbol
data.description:
Athira Pharma (ATHA) on Friday announced a change in name to LeonaBio and ticker symbol to LONA.

The name change aligns with the company's transformative acquisition of rights to develop and commercialize lasofoxifene, a clinical asset for the potential treatment of metastatic breast cancer.

Shares will start trading on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the new ticker symbol on January 12, 2026.

data.title: Polestar reports record retail sales in 2025
data.description:
• Polestar (PSNY) retail sales amounted to an estimated 15,608 cars in Q4 2025, up 27% versus Q4 2024, the company said on Friday.
  • For the full year, retail sales totalled approximately 60,119 cars, a growth of 34% compared to 2024.
  • “For retail sales volumes, 2025 has been the best year ever for Polestar, despite continued external headwinds and challenging market conditions," Michael Lohscheller, Polestar CEO said.
  • "We are gaining market share and outselling many established car brands across key European markets – a testament to the expansion of our sales network, which has grown by over 50% this year, our attractive model line-up and the team’s hard work.”
  • Shares in Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (PSNYW) were up ~8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Bruker secures $500M in multi-year MRI superconductor orders
data.description:
• The Bruker Energy & Supercon Technologies division, a segment of Bruker (BRKR), on Friday announced two multi-year supply agreements for Bruker’s latest superconductors, which are used by global radiology companies to build their next generations of magnetic resonance imaging magnets.
  • The combined order value of the two agreements is approximately $500 million in expected future BEST revenues, with different agreement duration periods, with one agreement extending up to seven years.
  • These recently concluded agreements represent expansions of previous superconductor supply framework agreements.
  • Bruker (BRKR) up 1.8% premarket at $53.41.
